key_insight: A very small, interpretable two-parameter model can reconstruct dynamical systems zero-shot by blending the current latent state with the nearest in-context neighbor and its next state.
engineering_relevance: The paper suggests that robust out-of-domain dynamical forecasting may be achieved with far fewer parameters and simpler mechanics, making deployment and analysis easier.
method: Iteratively reduce a strong FM baseline (DynaMix) to a minimal model called DynaBase.
Use a linear blend of the current latent state, nearest context neighbor, and its temporal successor to form forecasts.
Derive closed-form one-step prediction solutions and study a 1-parameter family of behaviors.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports highly competitive zero-shot reconstruction performance across chaotic and cyclic dynamical systems, with negligible parameter load and optimization/evaluation on reconstruction and MSE measures.
watch_item: Despite strong zero-shot results, the model’s simplicity may make performance sensitive to context quality, neighbor selection, and the choice of training objective.
paper_title: A Minimal Interpretable Architecture for Zero-Shot Reconstruction of Dynamical Systems
